message:
fix(saga,spawn,raptorq,tls): bug fixes and improvements across subsystems

Saga executor — two correctness fixes:
- Preserve first fallback_reason: when multiple steps in a batch
  violate monotonicity, only record the first violation rather than
  overwriting with each subsequent one, so the root cause is visible
- Fix coordination_free flag: a batch that started on the coordination-
  free path should report coordination_free=true even if a monotonicity
  violation triggers fallback for subsequent batches. The prior code
  incorrectly retroactively marked the executing batch as non-CF.
- Add regression test for first-violation preservation

Spawn macro — closure passthrough:
- Add syn "visit" feature to Cargo.toml
- When the spawn! input is already a closure expression, pass it
  directly to spawn_registered() instead of wrapping in a synthetic
  |__child_cx| closure, preserving user-specified parameter names
  and avoiding double-wrapping
- Add test for closure passthrough behavior

RaptorQ proof — truncation tracking:
- Set EliminationTrace.truncated = true when inactive_cols exceeds
  MAX_PIVOT_EVENTS, so proof comparison uses lenient matching for
  strategy_transitions when traces were truncated
- Pass exp_elim.truncated to compare_vec_lenient instead of hardcoded
  false, preventing false proof mismatches on large matrices

Cx::race — change empty futures behavior:
- Return pending().await instead of CancelReason error for empty
  futures vec, matching select/race semantics where no candidates
  means never resolving rather than immediate error

Rate limiter — remove dead Cancelled variant:
- Remove unused RejectionReason::Cancelled and its match arm from
  the combinator rate limiter; add rate_limit service tests

TLS acceptor — regenerate test certificates:
- Replace expired self-signed test cert/key with fresh 10-year certs
  including proper SAN (localhost), key usage, and EKU extensions

Artifacts — update golden checksums and raptorq wavefront pipeline
conformance data to match current behavior

Formatting — normalize line wrapping in distribution.rs and
quic transport.rs (rustfmt alignment)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 -----END PRIVATE KEY-----";
 
     #[test]
@@ -692,13 +692,13 @@ W7n9v0wIyo4e/O0DO2fczXZD
             )
             .await;
 
+            // Rustls 0.23 enforces RFC 7301: if both sides offer ALPN but there is no overlap,
+            // the server aborts the handshake with `no_application_protocol`.
             let client_err = client_res.unwrap_err();
-            assert!(matches!(client_err, TlsError::AlpnNegotiationFailed { .. }));
+            assert!(matches!(client_err, TlsError::Handshake(_)));
 
-            // Server is not configured to require ALPN, so it accepts the connection but
-            // no ALPN is negotiated.
-            let server = server_res.unwrap();
-            assert!(server.alpn_protocol().is_none());
+            let server_err = server_res.unwrap_err();
+            assert!(matches!(server_err, TlsError::Handshake(_)));
         });
     }
